speaker: Clinician
text: Good morning. To start, how has Liam been doing at home and school lately?

speaker: Parent
text: It's just been so overwhelming. He simply cannot sit still at dinner, he's constantly fidgeting. And when I try to talk to him, it's like he looks right through me, not at me. It breaks my heart.

speaker: Clinician
text: Liam, do you like sitting at the dinner table with mom?

speaker: Parent
text: He just lines up his toy cars for hours. If I move one, he has a complete meltdown. The teachers say he's easily distracted, but at home, he's hyper-focused on those cars.

speaker: Clinician
text: I see. The lining up of toys and distress at routine changes is notable. Does he respond when his name is called?

speaker: Parent
text: Barely. Half the time I think he has a hearing problem, but the pediatrician said his ears are fine. He just tunes us out entirely.
